_comment: Four PLCs on one subnet feeding the TASE.2 publisher. The gateway is a Modbus TCP MASTER: it connects OUTBOUND to each PLC's IP:port and polls the listed registers. Point the gateway at the PLCs here; you do not configure the PLCs to send anywhere. Each PLC must run a Modbus TCP server (slave) and expose these registers, and the gateway host must have a route to 10.30.0.0/24. Declare every PLC once under 'devices', then each tag references its device by name. Add a 5th PLC by adding a device + its tags and saving this file: the gateway reloads it live (no restart). NOTE: the current server publishes a fixed 4-point set (tm1/tm2/ts1/ts2), so this example uses one point per PLC; carrying more points per PLC needs the server's generalized tag model (see docs).

tags._note: MW from a 32-bit float in holding registers 0-1 on plc1
bus voltage: int16 in input register 100, scaled x0.1 to kV
transformer status word from input register 10 on plc3
breaker position (0/1) from holding register 5 on plc4
